content_en:
Muhammad Abd ar-Rahman ibn Abd ar-Rahim al-Mubarakpuri was born in 1283 AH (1866 CE) in the town of Mubarakpur in the Azamgarh district of northern India. He grew up in an era of intense Islamic scholarly activity on the subcontinent, when Indian hadith scholars were producing some of the most technically rigorous works of hadith commentary seen in centuries. His early education was obtained at local institutions, after which he traveled widely to study under the leading hadith scholars of his generation. He received his formative training in hadith sciences at the feet of masters who traced their chains of transmission back through the great Muhaddithun of Arabia and the Hejaz.

Al-Mubarakpuri spent the better part of his adult scholarly life teaching at Dar al-Ulum Mawdudiyyah and later at Dar al-Ulum Dar al-Hadith al-Rahmaniyyah. His teaching career gave him intimate familiarity with the texts and traditions of Islamic scholarship, and his students testify to his extraordinary memory for hadith and his meticulous attention to the conditions of narrators.

Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi bi-Sharh Jami' at-Tirmidhi is al-Mubarakpuri's magnum opus, written as a comprehensive commentary on the Jami' of Imam at-Tirmidhi. The work grew from the author's conviction that the Jami' had not received the sustained, technically rigorous commentary it deserved, given that it occupies a unique position in the hadith corpus. At-Tirmidhi's collection is distinguished from other major Sunan collections by its explicit grading of each hadith, its recording of the views of the major legal schools regarding each narration, and its discussion of narrator reliability. These features make it invaluable for the student of fiqh as well as the student of hadith.

Al-Mubarakpuri set out to explain each hadith, identify its chain of narrators, discuss the legal positions derived from it, reconcile apparent contradictions with other hadiths, and provide the historical and linguistic context necessary for proper understanding. The result was a work that runs to ten large printed volumes and stands as the definitive modern commentary on the Jami' at-Tirmidhi.

The author drew extensively on earlier commentaries and works of narrator criticism, synthesizing the opinions of scholars across the madhabs while maintaining the methodology of the hadith specialists. He died in 1353 AH (1934 CE), having completed this monumental work and established himself as one of the foremost hadith scholars of modern India.
content_id:
Muhammad Abd ar-Rahman ibn Abd ar-Rahim al-Mubarakpuri lahir pada 1283 H (1866 M) di kota Mubarakpur di distrik Azamgarh di India Utara. Ia tumbuh dalam era aktivitas keilmuan Islam yang intens di anak benua tersebut, ketika para ulama hadis India sedang menghasilkan beberapa karya komentar hadis yang paling ketat secara teknis dalam beberapa abad. Pendidikan awalnya diperoleh di institusi-institusi lokal, setelah itu ia melakukan perjalanan luas untuk belajar di bawah para ulama hadis terkemuka di generasinya. Ia menerima pelatihan hadis formatifnya di bawah bimbingan para master yang menelusuri silsilah transmisi mereka kembali melalui para Muhaddits besar Arabia dan Hijaz.

Al-Mubarakpuri menghabiskan sebagian besar kehidupan dewasa keilmuannya mengajar di Dar al-Ulum Mawdudiyyah dan kemudian di Dar al-Ulum Dar al-Hadith al-Rahmaniyyah. Karir mengajarnya memberinya keakraban mendalam dengan teks-teks dan tradisi keilmuan Islam, dan para muridnya bersaksi tentang ingatannya yang luar biasa untuk hadis dan perhatiannya yang teliti terhadap kondisi para perawi.

Tuhfat al-Ahwadhi bi-Syarh Jami' at-Tirmidzi adalah magnum opus al-Mubarakpuri, ditulis sebagai komentar komprehensif atas Jami' Imam at-Tirmidzi. Karya ini tumbuh dari keyakinan pengarangnya bahwa Jami' belum menerima komentar yang berkelanjutan dan ketat secara teknis yang layak diterimanya, mengingat posisi uniknya dalam korpus hadis. Koleksi at-Tirmidzi dibedakan dari koleksi-koleksi Sunan utama lainnya oleh penilaian eksplisitnya atas setiap hadis, pencatatannya tentang pandangan mazhab-mazhab hukum utama mengenai setiap riwayat, dan diskusinya tentang keandalan perawi. Fitur-fitur ini menjadikannya sangat berharga bagi penuntut ilmu fikih maupun ilmu hadis.

Al-Mubarakpuri bertujuan untuk menjelaskan setiap hadis, mengidentifikasi rantai perawinya, mendiskusikan posisi-posisi hukum yang diambil darinya, merekonsiliasi kontradiksi yang tampak dengan hadis-hadis lainnya, dan menyediakan konteks historis dan linguistik yang diperlukan untuk pemahaman yang tepat. Hasilnya adalah sebuah karya yang terdiri dari sepuluh volume cetak besar dan berdiri sebagai komentar modern definitif atas Jami' at-Tirmidzi.

Pengarang mengandalkan secara ekstensif komentar-komentar terdahulu dan karya-karya kritik perawi, mensintesis pendapat-pendapat ulama di berbagai mazhab sambil mempertahankan metodologi para spesialis hadis. Ia wafat pada 1353 H (1934 M), setelah menyelesaikan karya monumental ini dan menetapkan dirinya sebagai salah satu ulama hadis terkemuka India modern.
